oscar.best_picture.note: Crash wins Best Picture in a high-profile upset over Brokeback Mountain, which had won Best Director and most major precursor awards. The result remains one of the most-debated Best Picture choices of the 21st century — critics later cited it as illustrative of homophobic bias in the older Academy membership.
oscar.best_picture.source: 78th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Ang Lee becomes the first Asian filmmaker — and the first non-white director ever — to win Best Director. Picture/Director split: Brokeback Mountain took Director, Adapted Screenplay, and Score; Crash took Picture, Original Screenplay, and Editing.
oscar.best_director.source: 78th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Philip Seymour Hoffman's first and only Oscar (he died in 2014). Won for portraying Truman Capote.
oscar.best_actor.source: 78th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Reese Witherspoon's first Oscar, for portraying June Carter Cash (with live vocal performance).
oscar.best_actress.source: 78th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: George Clooney's first acting Oscar; he was simultaneously nominated for Best Director and Best Original Screenplay for Good Night, and Good Luck — only the second person ever (after Orson Welles for Citizen Kane) to be nominated for directing, writing, and acting in two different films at the same ceremony.
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 78th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Rachel Weisz's first Oscar, on her first nomination.

oscar.best_original_song.source: 78th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: The Departed wins Best Picture, with Martin Scorsese taking Best Director — ending the most-discussed Best Director drought in modern Oscar history. The film won four Oscars total (Picture, Director, Adapted Screenplay, Editing).
oscar.best_picture.source: 79th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Martin Scorsese's first Oscar after five prior Best Director nominations (Raging Bull, 1981; The Last Temptation of Christ, 1989; Goodfellas, 1991; Gangs of New York, 2003; The Aviator, 2005), plus earlier nominations across writing and producing categories. The win was widely framed as career-overdue recognition; Steven Spielberg, Francis Ford Coppola, and George Lucas presented the award together.
oscar.best_director.source: 79th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Forest Whitaker's first Oscar, for portraying Idi Amin.
oscar.best_actor.source: 79th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Helen Mirren's first Oscar, for portraying Queen Elizabeth II — at the same ceremony as her two Emmy and SAG wins for portraying Queen Elizabeth I (Elizabeth I miniseries).
oscar.best_actress.source: 79th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Alan Arkin's first Oscar after 40 years and three nominations.
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 79th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Jennifer Hudson's first Oscar — for her feature film debut. Hudson was a former American Idol contestant; one of a small number of Oscar winners with this kind of debut performance.
oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 79th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 79th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 79th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.note: Gustavo Santaolalla's second consecutive Best Original Score (after Brokeback Mountain, 2006) — only the third composer to win in consecutive years (also John Williams and Alan Menken).

oscar.best_original_song.note: Melissa Etheridge's first Oscar — for the climate-change documentary An Inconvenient Truth. The film also won Best Documentary Feature at the same ceremony.

oscar.best_animated_feature.note: Happy Feet (Warner Bros / Animal Logic) — a non-Pixar/non-Disney winner of Best Animated Feature, breaking a string of Pixar/DreamWorks dominance. George Miller's first Oscar (he later won Best Picture for Mad Max: Fury Road indirectly via the production team).
oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 79th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.note: Germany's first International Feature Oscar in the modern era (most prior German wins were before reunification in 1990).
oscar.best_international_feature.source: 79th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_visual_effects.winner_film: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest

oscar.best_picture.note: No Country for Old Men sweeps the top categories: Picture, Director (co-direction), Adapted Screenplay, and Supporting Actor. The 80th-anniversary ceremony.
oscar.best_picture.source: 80th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Joel and Ethan Coen become the second co-directing team to win Best Director (after Robert Wise & Jerome Robbins for West Side Story, 1962) — until Daniels (Kwan & Scheinert) at cer 95. The Coens also won Adapted Screenplay at the same ceremony, taking three Oscars personally.
oscar.best_director.source: 80th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Daniel Day-Lewis's second Best Actor Oscar (after My Left Foot, 1990). His third would come at cer 85 for Lincoln, making him the only male performer with three Best Actor wins.
oscar.best_actor.source: 80th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Marion Cotillard wins Best Actress for portraying Édith Piaf — the first Best Actress win for a primarily French-language performance, and the first French-born winner of the category since Simone Signoret (Room at the Top, 1959, who was Polish-born but French-naturalised).
oscar.best_actress.source: 80th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Javier Bardem becomes the first Spanish actor to win an acting Oscar — for portraying Anton Chigurh.
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 80th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 80th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.note: Diablo Cody's first Oscar — for her debut produced screenplay.
oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 80th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.note: CSV recipients_json contains parser artifacts: 'the novel' (generic) and 'Cormac McCarthy' (the source-novel author, who is not a recipient of the screenplay Oscar) captured alongside the canonical Coen credits. oscar.best_original_song.note: Glen Hansard and Markéta Irglová win Best Original Song for the indie film Once — exceptionally rare for a small-budget independent musical to take the category. Markéta Irglová was 19 at the time, among the youngest Oscar winners in the category.
oscar.best_original_song.source: 80th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.note: Brad Bird's second Best Animated Feature (after The Incredibles, 2005). Pixar's third win in the category.
oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 80th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.source: 80th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_production_design.source: 80th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: Slumdog Millionaire wins eight Oscars from ten nominations — the third-highest haul for any Best Picture winner since 2000. The first Best Picture winner with a predominantly Indian setting and substantial Hindi-language dialogue. Final ceremony of the fixed 5-nominee Best Picture slate before the 82nd's expansion to 10.
oscar.best_picture.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Danny Boyle's first Oscar.
oscar.best_director.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Sean Penn's second Best Actor Oscar (after Mystic River, 2004) — for portraying Harvey Milk.
oscar.best_actor.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Kate Winslet's first Oscar after six prior nominations spanning 13 years (Sense and Sensibility, 1996; Titanic, 1998; Iris, 2002; Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind, 2005; Little Children, 2007 — plus The Reader at this ceremony). At 33, the youngest performer to receive a sixth acting nomination.
oscar.best_actress.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Heath Ledger's posthumous Best Supporting Actor win for his portrayal of the Joker — the second posthumous acting Oscar in history (after Peter Finch for Network, 1977) and the first posthumous win in the supporting categories. Ledger died in January 2008, before nominations were announced. Award accepted by his family. Some 13 years later, Joaquin Phoenix won Best Actor for portraying the same character (Joker, cer 92).
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Penélope Cruz becomes the first Spanish actress to win an acting Oscar (Javier Bardem had won the previous year as the first Spanish actor). The married pair are the first Spanish couple to both hold acting Oscars.
oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.note: A. R. Rahman's first Oscar — the first Indian composer to win Best Original Score, and the first composer in many years to win both Best Original Score AND Best Original Song at the same ceremony (also for Slumdog Millionaire's 'Jai Ho').
oscar.best_original_score.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_song.note: First Hindi/Urdu-language song to win Best Original Song. A. R. Rahman's second Oscar of the night. Slate of 3 nominees this year — within the typical=5±3 expectations tolerance.
oscar.best_original_song.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.note: Pixar's fourth Best Animated Feature in eight years.
oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.note: Japan's fourth International Feature Oscar (then Best Foreign Language Film) — following long-form winners across the prior decades.

oscar.best_visual_effects.winner_film: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button
oscar.best_visual_effects.source: 81st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: First ceremony of the expanded 10-nominee Best Picture slate (per AMPAS rule change announced in mid-2009). The Hurt Locker becomes the lowest-grossing Best Picture winner of the modern era at the time, beating Avatar — which had grossed roughly 100x more at the box office. Six Oscars total for The Hurt Locker.
oscar.best_picture.source: 82nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Kathryn Bigelow becomes the first woman to win Best Director in the 82-year history of the Academy. She also beat her ex-husband James Cameron (Avatar) for the prize — among the most-discussed competitive narratives of the season.
oscar.best_director.source: 82nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Jeff Bridges's first Oscar after five prior nominations spanning 38 years (The Last Picture Show, 1972; Thunderbolt and Lightfoot, 1975; Starman, 1985; The Contender, 2001; Crazy Heart at this ceremony).

oscar.best_actress.note: Sandra Bullock's first Oscar nomination and win.

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Christoph Waltz's first Best Supporting Actor — for portraying Hans Landa. He became the first Austrian-born actor to win an acting Oscar; his second came at cer 85 for Django Unchained, also a Tarantino film.
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 82nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.note: Up wins Best Animated Feature; it was simultaneously nominated for Best Picture (only the second animated film ever to receive a Best Picture nomination, after Beauty and the Beast at the 64th in 1992 — and the first under the expanded 10-nominee slate). Pete Docter's first Oscar; he later won again for Inside Out (cer 88) and Soul (cer 93).

oscar.best_international_feature.source: 82nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_production_design.source: 82nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: First ceremony of the variable 8-10 slate after the 82nd's fixed-10 expansion. The King's Speech wins Best Picture in a high-profile split with David Fincher's The Social Network, which had taken the season's earlier critical-precursor awards (NYFCC, Globes Drama, BAFTA). The King's Speech also took Director, Actor, and Original Screenplay — a four-Oscar haul.
oscar.best_picture.source: 83rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Tom Hooper wins Best Director over David Fincher (The Social Network) — the most discussed Picture+Director split of the season, with Fincher having swept earlier precursor awards including the Golden Globe and the DGA preliminary tally.
oscar.best_director.source: 83rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Colin Firth's first Oscar after his prior nomination (A Single Man, 2010).
oscar.best_actor.source: 83rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.source: 83rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Christian Bale's first Oscar nomination and win. Both Supporting Acting Oscars went to The Fighter (also Melissa Leo for Best Supporting Actress) — a rare same-film sweep of the supporting categories.

oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 83rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.note: David Seidler, 73, becomes the oldest Best Original Screenplay winner up to that point — for a screenplay he had been developing for decades.

Reznor (Nine Inch Nails) is the first major rock musician to win Best Original Score in the modern era. The Social Network lost Best Picture but won this category, in keeping with the Picture/Director split narrative of the night.

oscar.best_original_song.note: Randy Newman's second Best Original Song Oscar (after 'If I Didn't Have You' from Monsters, Inc., 2002). Slate of 4 nominees this year — within the typical=5±3 expectations tolerance.
oscar.best_original_song.source: 83rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.note: Toy Story 3 wins Best Animated Feature; at the time, the first animated film to gross over $1 billion worldwide. Pixar's seventh Best Animated Feature win in the category's nine-year history. Slate of 3 nominees per the pre-2012 lower-volume era.
oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 83rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.source: 83rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_visual_effects.source: 83rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

Pre-rename era of the category — same root cause as cer 88+ Production Design artifacts. See known-issue oscar-production-design-role-label-artifact-2026-05-03 (scope expanded to cover the older 'Art Direction' phrasing across cer 83-84).

oscar.best_picture.note: The Artist becomes the first largely silent (and predominantly black-and-white) film to win Best Picture since Wings won the inaugural award at the 1st ceremony in 1929 — an 84-year gap. Also the first French-produced Best Picture winner.
oscar.best_picture.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: First French director to win Best Director.
oscar.best_director.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Jean Dujardin becomes the first French actor to win Best Actor, and the first Best Actor winner for a (predominantly) silent performance since Emil Jannings at the inaugural ceremony in 1929.
oscar.best_actor.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Meryl Streep's third Oscar (after Best Supporting Actress for Kramer vs. Kramer, 1980, and Best Actress for Sophie's Choice, 1983) — and her first acting Oscar in 29 years across 14 nominations in the interim. Joins Ingrid Bergman as one of the only actresses with three competitive acting Oscars at the time (Katharine Hepburn at 4 was the all-time leader).
oscar.best_actress.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Christopher Plummer, 82, becomes the oldest acting Oscar winner up to that point (record subsequently broken by Anthony Hopkins at 83 in cer 93). His first Oscar after a 53-year film career.
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Octavia Spencer's first Oscar; sixth Black actress to win Best Supporting Actress.
oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.note: Woody Allen's third Best Original Screenplay (after Annie Hall, 1978, and Hannah and Her Sisters, 1987) — extending his record as the most-credited Best Original Screenplay winner.
oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.note: CSV recipients_json contains a parser artifact 'the novel' captured as a recipient (from the 'based on the novel by Kaui Hart Hemmings' clause). See known-issue oscar-adapted-screenplay-the-play-artifact-2026-05-03 (Batch C scope expansion).
oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.note: Robert Richardson's third Best Cinematography Oscar (after JFK, 1992, and The Aviator, 2005), making him a three-time winner alongside Conrad Hall and Vittorio Storaro.
oscar.best_cinematography.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.winner_film: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.note: Ludovic Bource wins Best Original Score for an effectively silent film — the score functions as the film's primary auditory layer, making this an unusually score-dependent win in the category's history.
oscar.best_original_score.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_song.note: Bret McKenzie's first Oscar. Famously the smallest Best Original Song slate in modern Oscar history — only 2 nominees, due to a quirk in the category's nomination rules in effect at the time. CSV subject_title contains markup-leak artifact ("'''[[Man or Muppet]]") instead of the clean song name; see known-issue oscar-original-song-subject-title-markup-leak-2026-05-03. Phase E winner_subject check still passes via fuzzy substring match.

oscar.best_animated_feature.note: Rango (Paramount Animation / Industrial Light & Magic) becomes a rare non-Disney/Pixar/DreamWorks Best Animated Feature winner — Pixar had won six of the prior eight Animated Feature Oscars.
oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 84th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: Argo wins Best Picture despite Ben Affleck not receiving a Best Director nomination — only the fourth time in Oscar history (and first since Driving Miss Daisy, 1989) that the Best Picture winner was directed by someone not nominated for Best Director. oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Anne Hathaway's first Oscar; widely credited to her live-on-set vocal performance of 'I Dreamed a Dream'.

oscar.best_original_screenplay.note: Quentin Tarantino's second Best Original Screenplay (after Pulp Fiction, 1995).

oscar.best_actor.note: Matthew McConaughey's first Oscar nomination and win, capping the 'McConaissance' career reinvention from romantic-comedy lead into prestige dramatic actor.

oscar.best_actress.note: Cate Blanchett's second Oscar (after Best Supporting Actress for The Aviator, 2005) and first Best Actress.

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Lupita Nyong'o's first acting role and first Oscar — the first Kenyan and the first Mexican-born actor to win an acting Oscar, and the seventh Black actress to win Best Supporting Actress.

oscar.best_original_screenplay.note: Spike Jonze's first Oscar — sole-credit win for an original screenplay across an unusually competitive year (also nominated: American Hustle, Blue Jasmine, Dallas Buyers Club, Nebraska).

oscar.best_international_feature.note: South Korea's first International Feature Oscar. First film ever to win both Best Picture and Best International Feature at the same ceremony — a precedent only matched once again (CODA was not an International nominee). The category was renamed from 'Best Foreign Language Film' to 'Best International Feature Film' at this ceremony, making Parasite the first winner under the new category name.
